w:r.w:t: ECOLEARN : GAMIFIED ENVIRONMENTAL EDUCATION FOR CLIMATE ACTION
EcoLearn : Gamified Environmental Education for Climate Action
in partial fulfilment of the requirements for
the award of the degree of
authentic record of our own work carried out during the period from Jul-2025 to Dec-2025 under the supervision of
The content presented in this project has not been submitted by me for the award of any other degree elsewhere.
We would like to express our sincere thanks
Gandhi Nagar, Bhopal for giving us an opportunity to undertake this project.
We also take this opportunity to express a deep sense of gratitude to
Prof. Nargish Gupta, HOD, Department of Computer Science & Engineering
us with the right guidance and advice at crucial junctures and for showing us the right way.
guidance at every point of time.
project whenever I found myself in any issue.
Agriculture is the backbone of the economy, and crop diseases are a major cause of reduced agricultural
treatment of plant diseases are essential for improving crop yield and ensuring food security; however, traditional disease detection methods rely on manual observation and expert consultation, which

image processing techniques to identify crop diseases accurately by analysing uploaded leaf images. In addition to disease detection, the system provides detailed diagnostic information such as symptoms, confidence score, treatment recommendations, and preventive measures, along with real-time weather integration to support better agricultural decision-making. The platform also includes secure user authentication and maintains a history of previous detections for long-term crop monitoring. Developed using modern web technologies such as React, Flask, and Firebase, this system promotes smart and sustainable agriculture by reducing crop losses, improving decision-making, and supporting digital transformation in the agricultural sector.
Agriculture plays a vital role in sustaining human
life and economic development. One of the biggest challenges
affect crop quality and yield if not treated at an early stage. In many rural and semi-urban areas, access to agricultural
challenges by providing an intelligent, automated, and user-friendly solution for crop disease identification.
interface. The uploaded images are processed using Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning models to detect diseases accurately.

The system not only identifies diseases but also provides comprehensive diagnostic information, including symptoms, treatment methods, and preventive measures. Additionally, real-time weather information is integrated into the platform to assist users in agricultural planning.
detection history feature allows users to track previous analyses, supporting long-term crop health monitoring.
inspection, minimizes crop losses, and promotes sustainable farming practices. The system aligns with modern digital
AI-Powered Crop Disease Detection for Food Security
diseases. The specific objectives of the project are as follows:
To provide accurate disease identification along with confidence scores and detailed diagnostic
detecting plant diseases accurately, and providing an interactive and user-friendly platform for farmers and agricultural users.
records, weather details, and detection history. Its cloud-based scalability and real-time synchronization support efficient data management and fast access for agricultural users.
application. The specific requirements are as follows:
higher RAM: Minimum 4 GB Storage: Minimum 256 GB

In the agricultural sector, crop diseases remain one of the major causes of reduced crop yield and economic loss for farmers. Traditional methods of disease detection largely depend on manual observation, expert consultation, and laboratory testing, which are time-consuming, costly, and not easily accessible, especially in rural areas. Due to the lack of a centralized and automated disease detection system, identifying plant diseases at an early stage becomes difficult, leading to delayed treatment and increased crop damage.

history, and environmental conditions, while agricultural experts face difficulties in providing timely support to a large number of farmers. These limitations restrict effective decision-making related to disease control, crop management, and resource utilization.
digital system that can automatically detect crop
easily and receive accurate diagnostic information along with treatment recommendations. This project
AI-based, and user-friendly web application aimed
at improving agricultural productivity, disease management, and sustainable farming practices.
The traditional crop disease identification process is largely manual, time-consuming, and dependent on
and intelligent web-based system that automates crop disease detection using artificial intelligence. The system

results along with symptoms, treatment suggestions, and preventive measures. It will also provide real-time weather information and maintain a history of previous detections. By streamlining disease diagnosis and providing timely information, the system supports better agricultural decision-making, reduces crop loss, and promotes smart farming practices.
AI-Powered Crop Disease Detection for Food Security
aim to address the above problems by providing an intelligent, efficient, and farmer-friendly solution. The objectives of the project are:
To provide detailed disease information including symptoms, treatment methods, and preventive

The project aims to develop a secure, web-based platform for automated crop disease detection using artificial intelligence. It allows users to upload images of crop leaves and receive instant diagnostic results. The system provides disease details, treatment recommendations, and preventive suggestions along with weather-based agricultural insights. A detection history feature enables long-term monitoring of crop health. The platform is scalable and can be extended to support multiple crop varieties, making it suitable for modern digital agriculture and sustainable farming initiatives.
face the following challenges and constraints:
AI models depends on the quality and diversity of training
The project is expected to deliver a reliable and user-friendly
AI-based web application that simplifies crop disease detection. Farmers and agricultural users will be able to identify diseases early, receive accurate
user data. Overall, it will promote efficiency, data-driven agricultural decisions, and support the vision of smart and sustainable farming.
Studies Studies in the domain of
smart agriculture and crop disease management

emphasize the need for technology-driven and automated disease detection mechanisms. Traditional methods of identifying crop diseases rely heavily on manual inspection, expert consultation, and visual observation, which often lead to delays, inaccuracies, and inconsistent diagnosis. Literature indicates that AI-based disease detection systems provide more reliable results through automated image analysis, centralized data storage, and standardized classification techniques. Such systems enable farmers to identify plant diseases at an early stage, monitor crop health efficiently, and take timely preventive actions. These findings highlight the importance of adopting an intelligent digital solution that minimizes manual dependency, improves diagnostic accuracy, and streamlines crop disease management processes for sustainable agriculture.[1]
highlights the importance of secure, web-based platforms for managing crop health data and user records. Studies point out that agricultural applications
and location-based data. Without adequate security measures, these systems may become vulnerable to unauthorized

secure authentication mechanisms, encrypted data storage, and controlled access policies to ensure data protection. Additionally, role-based interfaces and personalized dashboards significantly enhance usability by presenting relevant information to farmers and agricultural experts. These insights reinforce
authentication technologies and access control mechanisms while building a robust crop disease detection system.[2]
web and mobile platforms that enable seamless interaction between farmers, technology providers, and agricultural

simplicity, responsiveness, and quick access to diagnostic results through API-based communication. Such platforms allow users to upload crop images, receive instant disease analysis, and access treatment recommendations with minimal technical complexity. Research also underlines the advantages of modular UI components and reusable design patterns, which improve system consistency and reduce development complexity.
client–server communication, making them suitable for developing intelligent agricultural applications.[3]
precision agriculture and AI-driven farming solutions

time insights into crop health, generate actionable recommendations, and maintain historical disease records across multiple farming cycles. Literature shows that farmers benefit from structured dashboards that display detection history, weather conditions, and treatment guidance, while agricultural
components, and secure workflows to ensure long-term reliability and accessibility. These findings closely align with the objectives of the proposed
AI-Powered Crop Disease Detection for Food Security
, which aims to simplify disease diagnosis, enhance productivity, and promote sustainable farming through intelligent technology integration.[4]

perform. These requirements define the operations the system is expected to execute, ensuring it provides a seamless, efficient, and user-centric experience that fulfills its intended purpose of detecting crop diseases, providing diagnosis details, and supporting agricultural decision-making.
access disease detection features, view diagnosis results, and manage detection history. Users must also be able to log out securely from the system.
symptoms, treatment recommendations, and preventive measures.
Weather details should assist users in planning agricultural activities and preventing disease spread under unfavorable environmental conditions.

The system must store disease detection records for each user, including image details, detected disease, and date. Users should be able to view and manage previous detection results for monitoring crop health over time.
simple, intuitive, and easy to navigate, allowing farmers and agricultural users to interact smoothly with all system features.
in delivering accurate disease detection and agricultural insights.
The system shall automatically record user activities such as “Image Uploaded”, “Disease Detected”, and “Weather Data Viewed” to support tracking and analysis.

The AI-Powered Crop Disease Detection for Food Security is designed to meet several key non- functional requirements. These requirements are essential to ensure that the system performs efficiently, securely, and reliably under real-world agricultural usage conditions.

The performance of the AI-Powered Crop Disease Detection for Food Security is a critical non- functional requirement that directly affects user satisfaction and usability. The system is designed to ensure
high user load. It should efficiently handle concurrent requests without delays or timeouts.

Reliability ensures the system operates consistently with minimal downtime. The AI-Powered Crop Disease Detection for Food Security will include proper error handling, stable API integration, and recovery mechanisms to prevent failures and ensure continuous availability of services.

Usability focuses on providing an intuitive and user-friendly interface that allows farmers and agricultural users to use the system without technical difficulty. The system should require minimal training and offer clear instructions for all features.
secure authentication, encrypted communication, and strict access control. The system will safeguard user privacy and prevent unauthorized access.

Maintainability ensures the system can be easily updated, modified, and enhanced in the future. The application will follow a modular architecture and clear documentation to simplify debugging, maintenance, and future improvements.
The use case diagram for the
demonstrates the interaction between various actors and the system, highlighting the key functionalities and how they
interactions with the system and its major operational features.

web-based platform to identify crop diseases and monitor crop health. Users can register, log in securely, upload crop or leaf images, and receive disease diagnosis results along with treatment and prevention recommendations. Users can also view real-time weather information and manage their disease detection history for long-term crop monitoring.
and review detection reports. Admins can view logs, analyze usage patterns, and ensure the smooth functioning
AI-Powered Crop Disease Detection for Food Security
is designed using a three-tier architecture to ensure scalability, maintainability, and clarity. The overall architecture separates presentation, application logic, and data management layers.
The frontend provides a user-friendly interface for user registration, login, image upload, and result visualization.
images, view disease diagnosis, access weather data, and manage detection history efficiently.

The backend handles API requests, processes uploaded images, and communicates with AI-based disease detection services. It manages secure user authentication, role-based access control, activity logging, and integration with external
APIs to ensure accurate and secure data processing.
The database stores structured data such as user profiles, disease detection records, weather details,
and activity logs. Cloud-based storage is used for scalability and real-time data synchronization.
real- time weather information and supplementary agricultural data.
APIs. Processed results are returned to the frontend for display.

identification by analyzing plant leaf images. The ML module assists farmers and agricultural users by accurately detecting diseases at an early stage, enabling timely treatment and preventive action to reduce crop losses and improve yield.

Traditional crop disease detection methods rely on manual observation and expert knowledge, which can be time-consuming, inconsistent, and inaccessible to many farmers. These methods often lead to delayed diagnosis and improper treatment. To overcome these limitations, the ML module uses supervised
By training the model on labeled plant disease datasets, the system can generate accurate predictions, thereby improving reliability, consistency, and efficiency in crop disease diagnosis.
To achieve this, the system employs a
. This algorithm is chosen due to its simplicity, interpretability, and ability to capture linear relationships between multiple academic performance metrics and the final appraisal score.
to overall faculty performance, which are later used to predict outcomes for new faculty inputs.
TensorFlow/Keras, NumPy, OpenCV, and Flask, implementing a complete workflow from data preprocessing to
The dataset contains labeled images of healthy and diseased crop leaves representing various plant diseases.
dataset is prepared using image processing techniques to enhance learning accuracy.
A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model is trained to classify crop diseases. The dataset is divided into training and
model is trained over multiple epochs to optimize classification accuracy.
Model performance is evaluated using accuracy and loss metrics. Predictions made by the model are compared with actual labels to validate reliability and effectiveness in disease classification.
use. This allows the system to load the trained model without retraining, ensuring faster deployment.
API receives crop images through HTTP
requests, preprocesses them, passes them to the trained model, and returns disease prediction results in JSON format.
through a Flask API, the system enables real-time disease detection for users. The results validate the effectiveness
potential for future enhancements using larger datasets and advanced deep learning techniques.
for developing a responsive and user- friendly frontend interface. User authentication is implemented using
to ensure secure access to the system.
After successful login, users can upload crop or leaf images through
The backend processes the images and forwards them to the
AI-based disease detection module for analysis. Detection results, including disease name, confidence score, symptoms, and treatment recommendations,
data and detection history are also saved for future reference.
All recent user activities such as image uploads and disease detections are logged and displayed dynamically on the user dashboard. The dashboard
insights, ensuring an interactive and informative user experience.

At PlantGuard, we're committed to revolutionizing agriculture through technology.
to protect their crops from diseases.
By leveraging advanced artificial intelligence and machine learning, we help farmers
decisions that lead to healthier crops and better yields.

'text-gray- 500'}`}>Start by analyzing your first crop image.
Higher Education Career Advancement & Monitoring System

" demonstrates a functional and efficient digital platform for managing faculty self-appraisals. The frontend, developed using Next JS, Tailwind CSS, and TypeScript, provides a clean and responsive interface for seamless user interaction. JWT Authentication enables secure and role based login functionality for faculty members and administrators.

The developed web-based application streamlines the process of recording, verifying, and displaying faculty achievements such as publications, projects, and seminars. By integrating JWT for authentication and database management, the system ensures scalability, reliability, and ease of use. The
addresses key challenges in academic data management and provides a robust platform for maintaining transparency and academic integrity in higher education institutions.
While the current version of the system is functional, there are several potential improvements and extensions that can enhance its capabilities.
Integrating data visualization to show trends in research output, collaboration networks, and indexing
APIs Integration Vivek Kumar : Frontend Designing & Development Yash Raj : Backend Development, Machine learning.

improved crop health and sustainable farming practices..

features such as image upload, disease detection, and weather integration added incrementally until the final application is completed.

development, the Incremental SDLC model was followed to incorporate

access, especially for farmers in rural areas. This project presents a modern, AI-based web solution that automates

real-time diagnosis. The platform is built using React, Tailwind CSS, and JavaScript to create a user-friendly and responsive front-end interface that allows users to log in securely and upload crop images for analysis.
users can interact with the system..
